mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and drag down corporate revenues, producing negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real GDP growth indicates a synchronized supply-demand mismatch that erodes profit margins and triggers equity sell-offs over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or independent single-series models outperform the interaction model in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates fail to compress equity valuations because strong real GDP growth offsets the discount rate effect, resulting in positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in > 50% of tightening episodes, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with forward equity returns (p > 0.10), or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and depresses forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in > 65% of housing start collapses, or the cross-sectional IC remains below 0.04 across all rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real consumer spending declines alongside rising inflation, it indicates stagflationary pressures that compress equity risk premiums and drive negative forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 2-year window, or independent CPI or RSAFS models consistently outperform the joint specification.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, rising industrial production acts as a leading indicator for corporate earnings recovery, producing strong positive equity returns over a three-month horizon despite elevated interest rates.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no predictive power for forward equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or the signal reverses direction during actual recovery periods.
